title: Number of Ways to Stay in the Same Place After Some Steps
hints: Notice that the pointer can only reach indices up to steps. If arrLen is larger, treat it as steps + 1 to limit the state space.
Use dynamic programming where dp[i][j] represents the number of ways to be at index j after i steps.
For each step, transition from previous positions: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + dp[i-1][j] + dp[i-1][j+1], handling boundaries carefully.
